title: Duke faves, Michigan next in '27 hoops title odds
content: Duke is favored to win the 2027 men's basketball national championship, according to bookmakers, with newly crowned '26 champion Michigan second in next year's title odds.

title: Contreras warns Brewers: Hit me again, you'll pay
content: Willson Contreras had a warning for the Brewers after he was hit by a pitch for the 24th time by the team Monday. "... Next time they hit me again, I'm going to take one of them out. That's a message."

title: Bears HOFer McMichael, who died at 67, had CTE
content: Steve 'Mongo' McMichael died in 2025 at age 67 after a five-year battle with ALS, and his wife Misty donated his brain to research.

title: Lady Vols lose recruit Edwards, see roster cut to 1
content: On the same day that Tennessee guard Jaida Civil announced she's entering the transfer portal, Oliviyah Edwards, the No. 2 recruit in the SC Next 100 class of 2026, requested a release from her national letter of intent with the Lady Volunteers on Monday.

title: Cooper irked as penalty box injury sparks probe
content: The NHL is looking into the circumstances that led to Tampa Bay Lightning forward Pontus Holmberg hurting his left arm when he crashed shoulder-first into an unlatched penalty box door in Monday night's 4-2 loss to the Buffalo Sabres at KeyBank Center.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: A do-or-die battle for major fronts, their skippers
content: Having been in the political wilderness for a decade, the UDF  cannot afford to sit out of power for another five years. For the LDF, which suffered a series of setbacks in the Assembly bypolls, the Lok Sabha elections, and the local body polls, the April 9 Assembly elections will be a test of its credentials.  For the National Democratic Alliance too the polls will be decisive, having seen a slump in their vote share in the local body elections

content: They express apprehensions about the proposed creation of a designated authority to take provisional and, in many cases, permanent control of all foreign contributions and assets created — even partly — from such funds

title: IGMC Stadium in Vijayawada set for ₹280 crore renovation in phases
content: Minister for Transport and Sports Mandipalli Ramprasad Reddy to lay the foundation for phase-1 works on April 8.

content: BJP MP Ravi Shankar Prasad accuses Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge of making such a statement out of hatred for Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

content: The custodial torture was meted out to the father and son in June 2020, after they were detained at the Sattankulam police station on the mere charge of violating COVID-19 lockdown norms.

title: Watch: No cash at highway toll plazas from April 10
content: Cash payments at toll plazas will be discontinued for travellers on National Highways from April 10, 2026. In cases where a vehicle enters a toll fee plaza without a valid FASTag, users can still pay via Unified Payment Interface (UPI), but at a higher charge of 1.25 times the applicable toll fee.

title: IMA expresses concern on political parties’ support for ‘mixopathy’ in manifestos in Kerala
content: IMA issues statement on World Health Day, which is being observed on the theme ‘Unite for Health, Stand with Science’ this year

title: India issues advisory for nationals in Iran to stay 'where they are' for 48 hours
content: Indian embassy urged its nationals in Iran to remain indoors 'where they are' for 48 hours, avoiding specific installations, following escalating regional tensions. The advisory emphasizes staying put, especially in Embassy-hired hotels, and maintaining contact with embassy teams. This comes amid heightened warnings and reported strikes targeting key Iranian infrastructure.

title: Where should you put your money amid US-Iran war? 5 experts answer
content: It’s the start of a new financial year, and if you are wondering where to put your money - this article has you covered. We asked five experts what they think is the right portfolio strategy for investors at the current juncture.

title: Microsoft responds to viral claims on Copilot's terms of service language
content: Microsoft clarified that its Copilot Terms of Use, which previously stated the AI was "for entertainment purposes only," contained legacy language. The company explained this phrasing is no longer reflective of Copilot's integration into productivity and enterprise workflows, and will be updated soon. This clarification addresses concerns raised online about the AI's intended use.

title: World Health Day: AI doctors vs real doctors — where machines win and where they fail
content: AI tools demonstrate impressive accuracy in medical queries but struggle with context, as seen in a doctor's test. While AI excels at speed and data analysis, human doctors remain crucial for empathy, judgment, and building trust. The future of healthcare lies in a collaborative approach, leveraging AI to enhance, not replace, human medical expertise

title: Kerala's Ezhava equation: Is the Left losing its loyal Hindu base?
content: Long before electoral arithmetic began to matter, the Ezhavas were fighting for something more fundamental. Recognition, dignity and access. In 1896, a petition signed by 13,176 members of the community was submitted to the Travancore maharaja, protesting their exclusion from government jobs. It was not merely a grievance letter. It was an early articulation of collective political consciousness.

title: 'Akele batting kar lunga': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of Mumbai Indians clash
content: Young batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, known for his aggressive 'see the ball and tonk the ball' approach, is set to face Jasprit Bumrah as Rajasthan Royals prepare to play Mumbai Indians. The 15-year-old has carried his impressive U19 World Cup form into the current season, showcasing remarkable strike rates and averages.

title: Canada has changed immigration rules; changing visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting from April 2026 with passport and citizenship fee increases, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain increased autonomy in immigration assessments, while rural employers benefit from expanded foreign worker quotas.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 20: Ranveer Singh film surges past Rs. 1,025 crore
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has scripted history as the first Hindi film to breach the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Globally, the thriller has amassed Rs. 1,622.72 crore, outperforming blockbusters like Jawan. Despite the IPL 2026 season and a 229-minute runtime, the film remains a powerhouse. To boost family viewership, makers have released updated prints with muted abuses and technical fixes. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs. 1,700 crore mark.

title: India EV sales hit record 24.5 lakh in FY26; cars jump 84%, 2- and 3-wheelers lead volumes
content: India's electric vehicle sales surged past 20 lakh units for the first time, driven by robust two- and three-wheeler demand. Electric cars also saw remarkable 84% growth. This milestone signifies a significant leap in the nation's green mobility transition, with all vehicle categories contributing to this impressive expansion.

title: AI replacing radiologists: Docs slam Nvidia, Anthropic CEOs for false info
content: America's largest public hospital system CEO is open to replacing radiologists with AI, citing potential for "major savings" in areas like breast cancer screening. This statement follows similar claims from AI leaders, but a neuroradiologist criticizes these views as "fiction" and "ignorance," arguing AI's current impact on radiology is minimal and questioning the motives behind such pronouncements.

title: Who is Menaka Guruswamy? SC lawyer behind Sec 377 verdict now India’s 1st openly queer MP
content: Senior advocate Menaka Guruswamy, a prominent lawyer in the Section 377 decriminalization case, has taken oath as a Trinamool Congress MP from West Bengal. She becomes India's first openly queer member of Parliament, vowing to uphold constitutional values of equality and non-discrimination.

title: India unlikely to revise fiscal deficit target despite Gulf crisis, capex stays priority
content: India remains committed to its fiscal deficit target for 2026-27, prioritizing capital expenditure despite Middle East tensions impacting oil prices. While austerity measures are considered, crucial infrastructure projects are protected. Officials suggest subsidy targeting and savings could offset added fiscal pressure, but economists warn of potential slippage if fuel prices remain capped.

title: Kittle linked to Cowboys move as 49ers eye $5M defensive upgrade
content: Jadeveon Clowney is a highly rated edge defender. He last played for the Dallas Cowboys. He is a potential target for the San Francisco 49ers. Their roster has injured players and the farewell of Bryce Huff. Clowney’s beyond impressive 2025 stats and PFF grades could bolster the Niners’ weakest in the league pass rush. However, he told KPRC 2 that he wants a reunion with Lamar Jackson in Baltimore.

title: Rory McIlroy marriage drama deepens as book reveals divorce twist
content: Rory McIlroy’s short-lived divorce from Erica Stoll in 2024 drew huge attention, especially with Amanda Balionis rumors. A new book by Alan Shipnuck shares inside details, but confirms no affair. The couple reunited within a month, calling it a “wake-up call.” Despite personal challenges, McIlroy continues to focus on golf with family support.

title: Joey Barton assault case: Kevin Lynch may lose eyesight in one eye
content: Former footballer Joey Barton has been remanded in custody after an alleged assault on Kevin Lynch at Huyton and Prescot Golf Club in Liverpool. Lynch suffered serious facial injuries and may lose sight in one eye. Barton and Gary O’Grady face grievous bodily harm charges. On the same day, a court also ordered Barton to pay damages to Eni Aluko.

title: J&K: Police bust deep-rooted LeT module; arrest Pak terrorist wanted for 16 years
content: Jammu and Kashmir Police have busted a major Lashkar-e-Taiba module, arresting five individuals including a Pakistani terrorist wanted for 16 years. The operation, spanning multiple states, recovered significant weaponry and uncovered the terror group's funding network. Investigations are ongoing, with more arrests anticipated as the deep-rooted interstate network is dismantled.

title: Evacuating the ‘Dudes’: How US pulled off a high-risk rescue of F-15 pilots in Iran
content: A daring two-day US Air Force mission unfolded over Iran after an F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down. Hundreds of personnel and nearly 176 aircraft executed a complex combat search and rescue, braving heavy fire to extract both stranded airmen. Deception tactics were crucial in locating the second crew member amidst a massive Iranian manhunt.

title: SIT: Self-styled godman Ashok Kharat made ‘divine power’ claims to sexually exploit women
content: A self-styled godman, Ashok Kharat, has been arrested for allegedly exploiting women using claims of divine power and threats of death or defamation. Investigators revealed he used superstition and rituals to extort money and sexually assault victims.

title: 'Standards should not be lowered': How Tharoor reacted to Kharge's Gujarat remark
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge's remarks about Kerala voters being "educated and clever" and unlike those in Gujarat have drawn sharp criticism from the BJP. Thiruvananthapuram MP Shashi Tharoor responded by urging a higher standard of political discourse, stating that "our standards should not be lowered to that of those who use bad language."

title: Home-cooked non-veg thali gets cheaper in March, veg meal cost stays flat: Report
content: Home-cooked thali costs saw a mixed trend in March 2026. Non-vegetarian thalis became cheaper by 1% year-on-year, driven by lower chicken prices. Vegetarian thalis, however, remained stable as a significant tomato price hike offset reductions in onions, potatoes, and pulses. Monthly costs also decreased for both.

title: TN polls: AIADMK’s Leema Rose Martin is the richest candidate with Rs 1,049 crore assets
content: Leema Rose Martin, AIADMK's Lalgudi candidate, is the wealthiest contender in the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. She declared assets totaling approximately Rs 1,049.56 crore. Her affidavit reveals significant movable and immovable properties. Martin's husband and son also have substantial declared assets. The candidate faces pending criminal cases, some related to tax and money laundering investigations.

title: Aaron Glenn’s New York Jets urged to land $9M Jacksonville Jaguars WR in trade package
content: Brian Thomas Jr’s rookie contract with the Jacksonville Jaguars is under scrutiny after a statistical dip in 2025 season. The dip potentially leads to a trade. Despite losing key players like Travis Etienne Jr. and Devin Lloyd, Jacksonville might leverage Thomas to acquire draft capital, something they have been doing for seasons now. The New York Jets, under Aaron Glenn, are rumored to be a trade option for Thomas. The trade package includes two first-round picks, something they already have and acquired in Sauce Gardner trade.

title: Will Gill play in GT vs DC IPL match? Sai Sudarshan gives big update on injury
content: "Shubman is coming along well. He'll be playing tomorrow," Sai Sudharsan confirmed Shubman Gill's participation in tomorrow's (Wednesday) match against Delhi Capitals, after the GT skipper missed the last game with a muscle spasm.

title: Coach Jon Cooper calls out NHL officials after Pontus Holmberg's injury during match
content: After a controversial play in an NHL game between the Tampa Bay Lightning and the Buffalo Sabres, there was a lot of tension, and head coach Jon Cooper strongly criticized the officials. Right after Pontus Holmberg hurt himself when the penalty box gate was open. The moment has led to discussions about the safety of players, the quality of officiating, and how these kinds of situations keep happening in important games.

title: ‘AA22xA6’ title to be revealed at 11 am on April 8; birthday treat for Allu Arjun fans
content: Allu Arjun and director Atlee's highly anticipated pan-Indian film, 'AA22xA6', will unveil its official title and first look poster on April 8th at 11 AM, coinciding with the actor's birthday. The announcement promises a grand spectacle, hinting at a mystery drama with a parallel universe concept, multiple timelines, and extensive visual effects. Deepika Padukone stars as the female lead.

title: Bullion weakens: Silver down Rs 2k, gold slips Rs 600 ahead of Trump’s Iran deadline
content: Gold and silver prices dipped in Delhi as investors grew cautious due to US President Trump's ultimatum to Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz. Escalating Middle East tensions and fears of military action are impacting bullion markets. Global gold saw a slight rise, but dollar strength and oil price surges are creating volatility.

title: PSL scare: Pakistan player loses consciousness after head blow, taken to hospital
content: The right-arm medium-fast bowler was taken to a nearby hospital for checks. The Geo Super report said that Mir Hamza Sajjad is now conscious and stable. As a precaution, a CT scan will be done to check for any injury.

title: NHL insider Craig Button says Toronto Maple Leafs GM search tied to Auston Matthews future
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs are about to have a very important offseason, during which they will make decisions about their leadership that will affect their future. Craig Button, an NHL insider, says that the search for a new general manager for the team will be closely linked to Auston Matthews' future. The organization has to make important decisions about its roster direction, coaching stability, and contract dynamics. All of that could change the way it competes.

title: Centre asks states to double daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers
content: States and Union Territories are directed to double the daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers, ensuring targeted distribution with Oil Marketing Company support. The Centre assures a stable overall LPG supply, dismissing reports of shortages at petrol pumps and LPG distributors.  Over 6.75 lakh 5-kg cylinders have been sold since March 23.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity benchmarks, Sensex and Nifty, staged a strong intraday recovery to close higher on Tuesday. Easing crude oil prices, positive global cues, and robust buying in IT stocks fueled the rebound. Despite initial weakness, markets ended firmly in the green, with IT stocks leading the gains.

title: Firing outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul; 1 gunman killed, 2 in custody— video
content: Gunfire erupted outside Istanbul's Israeli consulate as three assailants attacked, resulting in one gunman killed and two apprehended. Turkish authorities, suspecting links to a religion-exploiting terror group possibly affiliated with IS, are investigating. The consulate had been vacant for years, and no Israeli personnel were present. Two police officers sustained injuries during the intense shootout.

title: Gold price today: Yellow metal slips; check 24K, 22K city-wise rates
content: Gold futures dipped Tuesday as rising crude oil prices and Middle East tensions made investors cautious, impacting demand for the safe-haven asset. While futures saw a decline, retail gold rates presented a mixed picture across major Indian cities, with some experiencing slight increases and others minor drops.

title: Tamim Iqbal replaces Bulbul as BCB chief amid push for better BCCI ties
content: The current president of BCB, Aminul Islam Bulbul was removed by the country's National Sports Council (NSC), which formed an ad-hoc committee to run the day-to-day affairs till fresh elections are held.

title: David Warner arrested for drunk-driving in Australia
content: Australian cricketer David Warner faces serious charges after being apprehended for drunk-driving in Sydney. Pulled over by police, the 39-year-old's breath test allegedly registered a reading of 0.104, significantly exceeding the legal limit. He is now scheduled to appear in court next month following his arrest.

content: AI-enabled CCTV cameras will monitor polling stations in real time. If cameras are switched off or any suspicious activity is detected, repolling can be ordered immediately.

content: Foreign policy is not about short-term optics or daily narratives; it must be assessed over the long term. Pakistan's repeated attempts to project itself as a decisive global player must be seen in this context.

content: Article 28(1) of the Indian Constitution is unambiguous: no religious instruction shall be provided in any educational institution wholly maintained out of state funds. Police training schools, funded and run by the state, fall squarely within this framework.

title: Parents Charged After Toddler Enters Wolf Enclosure At US Zoo
content: The parents of a toddler who suffered a minor injury at a Pennsylvania theme park zoo after squeezing through a fence near a wolf enclosure and making contact with one of the animals have been charged with endangering the welfare of children, police

title: Indian Carriers Cancelled Over 10,000 International Flights Since Iran War: Aviation Ministry
content: "Flights, especially to Europe and North America by Indian carriers, have to take longer routes, which has increased travel time and associated cost," Asangba Chuba Ao said.

content: A Wall Street research firm’s analyst on site in the Strait of Hormuz reports partial shipping activity, challenging claims of a complete blockade.

title: Masters 2026: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .
